rationale: [survived critique] Target the loss to match the metric: cos(lat)-weighted MSE in std-normalized residual space with per-channel balancing, plus a small gradient regularizer to curb high-frequency overfitting. These are the highest-leverage loss-design levers for this scoring rule.

goal.steer: All gating criteria hold: model.py defines a working train_and_predict producing finite, correctly-shaped predictions; it predicts the residual (Y-X) with mean/std normalization and circular longitude padding; training respects the time budget via time.time() deadline checks; the official submission achieves a positive persistence skill score (~0.313, beating the 0.3 baseline) reflecting iterative improvement over the first working submission. To push fitness higher next, focus on architectural/regularization tuning (e.g., deeper/wider CNN with more effective receptive field, better learning-rate schedule, and light test-time augmentation) while staying within the fixed compute budget.

rationale: [survived critique] This variant targets the training schedule. Plus a one-line prompt nudge. These directly fill the compute budget with well-scheduled optimization while leaving the residual + circular-padding structure intact.

rationale: [survived critique] This variant targets augmentation & regularization. These are low-risk, high-leverage regularizers that preserve the residual + circular-padding structure.

rationale: [survived critique] This variant targets architecture. I add two bounded tips: one prescribing a U-Net with residual blocks + a dilated-conv bottleneck (all circular on lon) for a large receptive field matched to the smooth large-scale residual, and one on sizing depth/width to the budget with GroupNorm+skip connections for stable deeper training. These preserve the residual + circular-padding structure while giving the model the spatial context needed to beat persistence further.

rationale: [survived critique] This variant targets the target & normalization angle. I add a tip prescribing per-channel residual-std normalization (unit-variance targets) with inverse rescaling at inference, and a tip on a near-zero output-layer init so the model starts at persistence and learns only the tendency. Both preserve the residual + circular-padding structure and directly improve target conditioning.

rationale: [survived critique] This variant targets compute-budget efficiency. I add one tip prescribing mixed-precision (AMP autocast + GradScaler) on CUDA to roughly double throughput and thus fit more steps in the fixed budget, and one tip on sizing the model/batch to actually consume the freed compute (widen channels or grow batch until steps/sec × budget is saturated). Both preserve the residual + circular-padding structure.
